pi_items.source: OpenBCI GUI documentation confirms channels are numbered 1-8; task description states 'Verify that only channels 1, 2, 3, and 4 are active and visible'
Task description states 'Click buttons 5, 6, 7, and 8 to disable them. The waveform traces for these channels should disappear.'
Task description explicitly states 'save it as "four_channel_display.png"' and setup_task.sh removes 'four_channel_display.png' before task execution

OpenBCI documentation states 'Screenshots taken in the OpenBCI GUI using the lowercase m key will be saved to /Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/' and task description confirms 'the default Screenshots folder (~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/)'
Correct concatenation of output_dir and output_filename; confirmed in export_result.sh line 8: OUTPUT_PATH="/home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/four_channel_display.png"

privileged_info_summary: The disable_channels_5_to_8 task operates on OpenBCI GUI v5.2.2 with 8-channel EEG configuration. Metadata correctly specifies that channels 1-4 should remain active while channels 5-8 are disabled. The output filename and directory path are verified as correct locations for OpenBCI GUI screenshots (~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/). The task uses real EEG data from PhysioNet EEGMMIDB dataset (Subject S001, Run 01 - eyes open baseline) loaded during environment initialization, not synthetic data. The full output path correctly concatenates directory and filename.
